name: Hero Bar
description: A from-scratch, in-flow rotating featured-content hero for the Jellyfin home page, built as a lighter-weight, fully compatible replacement for the third-party Media Bar plugin. Inserted as a normal first child of the home tab content (not a body-level overlay), so it never overlaps or requires manual positioning fixes against other home rows. Mixes recently added items with a trending row, shows ClearLogo art with a text-title fallback, rating/year/genre metadata, overview, and Play/Info/Favorite buttons with clickable pagination dots.
overview: In-flow rotating hero for the home page - a from-scratch Media Bar replacement.

versions.changelog: Mobile + performance: touch swipe changes hero slides, with touch events contained inside the hero so Jellyfin's tab strip no longer hijacks a hero swipe into a tab switch (the swipe-to-Favoritter bug); vertical scrolling unaffected. Manual slide changes (dots, swipes) share the rotation's slide counter and restart its timer, fixing desynced auto-flips. Tailored phone layout: shorter banner, tighter text/logo sizing, tappable 40px buttons, larger dot hit areas. Performance: recently-added/trending pool cached 10 min in sessionStorage so the hero paints instantly on revisit; resume/next-up state always fetched fresh.

Visual: the hero now blends into the page background instead of ending in a hard box edge. The backdrop and tint layers sit behind a vertical alpha mask that fades them out at the top and bottom, letting ElegantFin's real background gradient (measured live: linear-gradient(0deg, rgb(28,30,34) 35%, rgb(37,39,45))) show through - a true blend with no color matching involved. Text, buttons, and pagination dots are outside the masked layer and stay fully crisp.

Fix: v1.0.0.0 could stack many duplicate heroes on the home page - the duplicate check ran synchronously but insertion happened after async data fetches, so every DOM mutation during that window started another insert; now guarded by a synchronously-set pending flag plus a re-check right before insertion. Play button now actually starts playback (remote-controls the client's own session via the Sessions PlayNow command) instead of linking to the details page, with details as fallback if the play command fails. Dynamic play button: shows Fortsæt and resumes where you left off for partially-watched movies, and for series in progress plays the half-watched episode (Resume) or the next unwatched episode (NextUp).

Initial release. Recently added + trending item mix (trending via the same Playback Reporting query New Badges' Trending row uses), ClearLogo image per slide with automatic text-title fallback when no logo art exists, rating/year/age-rating/genre metadata line, truncated overview, Play/Info/Favorite buttons, clickable pagination dots, and configurable slide count/rotation interval/trending toggle. Built as an in-flow first child of #homeTab (not a body-level overlay like Media Bar), so it never overlaps Mine medier/Continue Watching and needs no manual positioning fixes. Uses the verified ElegantFin header color (rgba(30,40,54,...)) for its gradient overlay so it blends into the header with no seam at any window width.

name: New Badges
description: Adds a date-accurate NEW badge to items in Recently Added home page rows (shown only for items added less than 7 days ago), shows the season/episode of a show's newest episode in place of its unwatched count for every still-airing show, forces item-detail backdrop images to render at any window width, replaces the Next Up row with a Trending row ranked by other users' recent plays, and merges Continue Watching with Next Up into one row.
overview: Real 'added < 7 days ago' NEW badges for Recently Added rows.

versions.changelog: Custom Langehub server logo in the header, replacing the default Jellyfin banner. The original export had the transparency checkerboard baked into the pixels (no alpha channel, 2172x724 / 774KB) - cleaned up with an edge-connected flood fill, cropped, and downscaled to 670x120 / 57KB with real transparency. Served from the plugin's own embedded resources (controller gained a binary path for PNGs), so no external image hosting is involved.

Two fixes and an optimization: (1) Trending and the merged Continue Watching row could duplicate after navigating around - the rendered-state flags reset when leaving home while Jellyfin kept the home DOM (and the inserted rows) mounted, so returning inserted a second copy; duplicate-detection is now DOM-based and can't drift out of sync. (2) The season/episode badge on Recently Added rows silently failed for shows also appearing in Trending (e.g. Silo showing its episode count instead of S3E2) - Trending's fetch pre-filled the date cache, making the badge scan skip the ongoing-status lookup the swap depends on; a series now only counts as cached once its ongoing status is known. (3) Ongoing-status lookups are one batched request per scan pass instead of one per show.

Two changes: (1) Removed the blue NEXT badge from next-episode recommendation cards in the merged Continue Watching row - those cards now render plain, no badge. (2) Performance: Trending and the merged Continue Watching row both made several sequential API calls on every home visit with no caching, making them visibly slower to appear than native rows. Both now cache their fetched data in sessionStorage with stale-while-revalidate (Trending: 10 min TTL, since its 30-day window barely changes; Continue Watching: 60s TTL, since Resume position changes while watching) - a home revisit, even across a full page reload, now paints instantly from the last-known data while a background refresh keeps it current. Also cut Trending's per-series date lookups roughly in half by skipping the ongoing-show check it never actually used (that's exclusively a Recently-Added-rows feature).

Fix: the merged Continue Watching row from 1.2.0.0 could get silently overridden - Jellyfin periodically re-fetches and re-renders the native Fortsæt afspilning row on its own (Resume state can change from other sessions/clients), which reset the inline display:none style we used to hide it, making the native row reappear a few seconds after our replacement loaded. Now re-asserted on every scan tick instead of only once at insertion time.

Two changes: (1) The Trending row's cards now get the same white hover-ring border and glare-sweep effect as every other native row - they were missing the card-hoverable class and an empty cardOverlayContainer element that those effects hang off of. (2) New feature: Continue Watching (Fortsæt afspilning) and Next Up (Næste afsnit) are merged into a single row. In-progress items keep their normal progress bar; shows whose last-watched episode is now fully finished get a blue NEXT card recommending the next unwatched episode instead of just disappearing from Continue Watching once you're caught up. Sourced from Users/{id}/Items/Resume and Shows/NextUp, deduplicated by series and sorted by last-played date.

New feature: replaces the Next Up home row with a Trending row, showing what other household members have watched in the last 30 days, ranked by distinct viewer count then play count. Sourced from the Playback Reporting plugin's own SQLite report DB via its submit_custom_query endpoint. Episode plays are resolved to their parent series so a show ranks as one unit. Horizontally scrollable like native rows (built on the real emby-scroller custom element), with a gold #rank badge on each poster - and the red NEW ribbon appears alongside it, rank first, when that item also qualifies as recently added. Falls back to leaving Next Up untouched if fewer than 4 distinct items are trending.

Fix: none of the badge features (NEW ribbon, episode-label badge) worked in Jellyfin's mobile layout mode. layoutManager.mobile is set purely from the browser's User-Agent string (not viewport width), and in mobile layout Recently Added rows render as a plain wrapping grid instead of a horizontal emby-scroller, so they never got the emby-scroller-container class our section filter required. The sectionN-exclusion heuristic itself still correctly identifies Recently Added rows on mobile - only the emby-scroller-container requirement was the blocker. Relaxed the filter to just .verticalSection plus the sectionN exclusion, which works identically on desktop and mobile.

New feature: force item-detail-page backdrop images to render at any window width. Jellyfin's own renderBackdrop() only sets the backdrop when innerWidth >= 1000px AND not in mobile layout, checked once at page load - below that it calls clearBackdrop() and never even requests the image, so no CSS override could ever fix this. This plugin now detects when the backdrop container is empty on an item details page and injects the backdrop image itself via the same ApiClient.getScaledImageUrl used natively, matching Jellyfin's own styling.

Expand the season/episode badge to every still-airing show in Recently Added rows, not just ones with an episode newer than 7 days. A show now gets the S{n}E{m} badge whenever its Status is Continuing (shown in the UI as "{year} - Nuværende"), regardless of when its latest episode was added. Ended shows keep the normal unwatched-count badge. The red NEW ribbon is unchanged and still only shows for episodes added in the last 7 days.

New feature: on Recently Added rows, when a show has a new episode (< 7 days old), the blue unwatched-count badge in the top-right corner now shows the season/episode of that new episode (e.g. S9E7) instead of the total unplayed count. Badge widened from a fixed circle to a pill shape so 4-character labels fit cleanly.

Fix: Shows/{id}/Episodes ignores SortBy/SortOrder and always returns episodes in season/episode order, so the 1.0.4.0 fix always resolved to episode S1E1 instead of the actual newest episode. Switched to Users/{userId}/Items?ParentId={seriesId}&IncludeItemTypes=Episode&Recursive=true&SortBy=DateCreated&SortOrder=Descending, which correctly honors the sort.

Fix: Series cards in Recently Added rows were checked against the series' own DateCreated (when the show was first added to the library) instead of the newest episode's arrival date, so a brand-new episode of a long-owned show never got a badge. Series-type cards now look up the latest episode via Shows/{id}/Episodes sorted by DateCreated.

Fix: File Transformation looks up an exact dictionary key for the requested file before falling back to regex matching, so our regex pattern 'index.html$' was never reached once another plugin (Media Bar) had already registered the literal key 'index.html'. Switched to the literal pattern 'index.html' so the badge script actually gets injected.
Fix: the file-transformation regex pattern was being double-escaped, so the injected script never actually landed in index.html. Badges now work.
Initial release.

name: Seerr Requests
description: Adds a 'Tilføj Film/Serie' button next to Hjem/Favoritter on the Jellyfin home page, opening a Seerr-backed panel to search for and request new movies/shows without leaving Jellyfin. Requests are attributed to the household member's own linked Seerr account when one exists.
overview: Search and request new movies/shows via Seerr, without leaving Jellyfin.

versions.changelog: Hover-expand card preview: hovering any browse/request card for a moment expands it into a larger preview with backdrop, rating/year/genres, a short description, a Læs mere button linking to the title's IMDb page (TMDB fallback), and the same Tilføj/status action including the Fortryd undo - desktop only, details fetched lazily via the new /SeerrRequests/media/{type}/{id} proxy and cached in-page. Language exclusion: new 'Excluded original languages' setting (comma-separated ISO 639-1 codes, prefilled with East/South/Southeast Asian languages) - browse rows, trending, and the upcoming hero hide matching titles, filtered server-side; explicit search is never filtered. Clear the field to show everything.

Mobile: touch swipe on the upcoming-releases hero, contained so Jellyfin's tab strip doesn't turn a hero swipe into a tab switch; manual slide changes stay in sync with auto-rotation and restart its timer. Genre pills collapse to one horizontally swipeable row on mobile instead of wrapping into several screen-eating rows. Search field pinned to 16px on mobile so iOS Safari stops auto-zooming on focus. Larger pagination-dot touch targets and a tighter phone-sized hero layout.

New: upcoming-releases hero on the Seerr tab. Thin rotating banner above the search field showing upcoming movie and TV releases (from Seerr's discover upcoming endpoints), soonest first, with a Danish release-date pill, title, short overview, and the same Tilføj request flow as the row cards including the Fortryd undo countdown - already-requested or available items show their status badge instead. Clickable pagination dots, 8s crossfade rotation, and the hero stays collapsed at zero height if Seerr is unreachable. New backend proxy endpoint /SeerrRequests/upcoming.

Found the actual root cause of the visible top seam the user kept reporting after two earlier attempts at the fade: it was a color mismatch, exactly as guessed, but the target color was wrong both times. Fetched ElegantFin's own CSS directly and found --headerColor:rgba(30,40,54,.8) (a blue-gray), used inside --headerColorGradient as a gradient that itself already fades to transparent by ~90% of the header's own height - the header was never black or Jellyfin's flat rgb(16,16,16) at all, it just looked that way at a glance. Both this tab's own top gradient and the intensified Hjem hero fade from 1.5.9.0 were using near-black instead, so the header's own fade and the hero/tab fade below it were two differently-toned overlays meeting at a hard edge. Switched both to the same rgba(30,40,54,...) - confirmed with zoomed before/after screenshots on both Hjem and this tab that the seam is gone and the two now read as one continuous fade.

First, ruled out the user's own suspicion that this plugin's CSS might be conflicting with Media Bar or New Badges - confirmed live by fully disabling this plugin's entire stylesheet and checking that Media Bar's hero position/appearance didn't change at all. It doesn't: Media Bar's own fade (.slide .gradient-overlay) is a diagonal-only wash (linear-gradient(130deg,...)), not a top-down fade, so the weak "fade at the top" feeling on Hjem was never a plugin conflict, just how Media Bar's own overlay happens to be designed. Layered a proper vertical dark-to-transparent gradient on top of (not instead of) that existing diagonal one, confirmed via a real screenshot to give Hjem's hero a much more pronounced top fade. Also tightened the Mine medier spacing fix from 1.5.7.0/1.5.8.0 - the +16px buffer used to clear the hero overlap left more empty space than wanted once the actual overlap was gone, brought down to +4px.

The tab-row down-shift and the top gradient still weren't showing up for the user despite two earlier attempts, and they were right - both were genuinely still broken. (1) margin-top on the tab row (tried at .6em then 1.6em) was being mostly absorbed by the header's CSS grid centering (align-items:center) - a computed 23.8px margin-top was only producing a ~2.6px actual visual shift, confirmed precisely via getBoundingClientRect before and after. Switched to position:relative + a plain pixel top offset instead, which isn't subject to that centering interaction at all - confirmed via a real before/after screenshot comparison that the row now visibly clears the logo instead of sitting inline with it. (2) The gradient looked like a floating rectangle instead of a page-wide fade because it was scoped to .sections, which is max-width:1400px + margin:0 auto - the gradient was cutting off at that box's own edges rather than reaching the sides of the window. Moved it to the tab element itself, which isn't width-constrained, so it now spans edge to edge the same way Hjem's own hero does behind its own padded content.

The user pointed out half-window mode already looked perfect (both the fade and the Mine medier spacing), which narrowed this down fast: at a ~1540px window Media Bar's hero (#slides-container) is 750px tall, and Mine medier's row was starting at 631px - a ~210px overlap where the row's own poster art was painting over the bottom of the hero. That's also why the fade looked "missing" specifically at wide windows - it wasn't gone, it was being covered. A fixed padding value couldn't fix this without breaking the already-correct narrower layout, so the row's position is now measured against the hero's actual bottom edge every tick (and on window resize) and only pushed down by exactly the overlap when one actually exists. Also: (1) fixed the Fortryd cancel button showing Anmodet instead of Tilfoej afterward even though the cancellation had genuinely succeeded on Seerr's side - Seerr's DELETE returns 204 No Content with an empty body, which the frontend's own resp.json() call was throwing on, turning a real success into a caught error. (2) Cards in the browse rows brought closer together again (gap now .3em, down from .6em) per feedback that they still felt too spaced out.

Corrections and a new feature: (1) 1.5.5.0 hid the entire Mine medier section by mistake - only the heading text is hidden now, the row of library cards underneath stays visible as intended. (2) The tab row down-shift from 1.5.4.0 was barely visible at full-window widths - confirmed live that .headerTabs is a CSS grid with the tab row centered inline with the logo there (unlike the narrower layout where it sits on its own line below), so a small margin got mostly absorbed. Increased from .6em to 1.6em, confirmed visibly correct at both a ~1540px and an ~800px window. (3) The decorative top gradient added for this tab in 1.5.4.0 turned out to be technically rendering but nearly invisible - too close in tone to the page's own background. Darker and more opaque now, actually reads as a fade. (4) Cards in Trending/Film/Serier/Seneste anmodninger were sitting further apart than intended, and hovering a card near a row's edge clipped its hover-ring glow against the row's own scrollable boundary - reduced the gap and added padding on all sides of each row so the ring has room to render. (5) New: pressing Tilfoej now shows a red Fortryd (undo) button with a live 5 second countdown before settling into the normal Anmodet state, so a mis-click can be reversed instead of leaving a real Seerr request behind. Backed by a new DELETE SeerrRequests/request/{id} endpoint proxying Seerr's own request-cancellation API.

Two more feedback items, shipped before the previous 1.5.4.0 update was even installed: (1) Hides the native Mine medier (My Media) home section - confirmed live this is actually a per-user-configurable Jellyfin home section (the section0 slot), matched here by its heading text rather than that slot class so it stays correct regardless of how sections are ordered. Worth knowing this can also be turned off directly in Jellyfin's own Display preferences > Home screen sections, no plugin needed - hidden here anyway since it was asked for directly. (2) The custom scrollbar added in 1.5.3.0 for Trending/Film/Serier/Seneste anmodninger turned out not to be wanted after all - the bar itself is now hidden (scrollbar-width:none / ::-webkit-scrollbar{display:none}) while the underlying overflow-x:auto scrolling stays fully functional via mouse-wheel, trackpad, touch, or drag.

Feedback round: (1) Removed the quality/season picker popup entirely - it never rendered reliably enough to be worth keeping, so Tilfoej now submits the request immediately at the fixed default (1080p, all seasons for TV) instead. Also removed the now-unused tv-seasons backend endpoint and Seasons request field. (2) The Behandles loading dots now bounce once per dot in quick succession near the start of a 5 second cycle, then go quiet until the next one, instead of bouncing continuously every ~1 second. (3) Confirmed live that Media Bar's own slideshow (a fixed element parented directly to body) only renders while the native Hjem tab content is actually active - it isn't broken by this plugin, it's correctly scoped to the real home tab and hides behind this one. Added a purely decorative dark-to-transparent gradient at the top of this tab instead, so it doesn't look as flat compared to Hjem's own hero backdrop. (4) The Jellyfin wordmark in the header (not a link anywhere in Jellyfin's own markup) now navigates back to Hjem when clicked, from any top-level page where it's shown - non-home pages already have Jellyfin's own native Hjem button in its place, so this only needed wiring where the static logo is what's actually visible.

Found that 1.5.2.0's fixed-delay retries (rAF + 50ms + 200ms) for cancelling the stuck dialog entrance animation still weren't a wide enough net - live testing showed Jellyfin's MutationObserver can attach the animation later than 200ms, so the dialog was still confirmed stuck at opacity:0 after the previous fix. Switched to a bounded polling loop (checks every 120ms for about 4 seconds, cancelling whenever an animation is found) which reliably catches it regardless of when it lands. Also five feedback-driven changes: (1) the whole Hjem/Favoritter/Tilfoej Film/Serie tab row is nudged down slightly instead of sitting flush against the very top edge. (2) Every card action state - the Tilfoej button and the Tilfoejet/Anmodet/Behandles badges - now shares one consistent bottom-center slot on the poster instead of badges living top-left while the button lived bottom-center. (3) Behandles now shows three small dots that bounce in sequence, a loading-style cue instead of a static label. (4) Trending/Film/Serier/Seneste anmodninger rows switched from the native is="emby-scroller" (which turned out to scroll via a JS transform, not real overflow - there was never an actual native scrollbar to restyle) to a plain overflow-x:auto row with a custom-styled indigo scrollbar, replacing the previous prev/next arrow buttons with real mouse-wheel/trackpad/touch scrolling plus a visible modern scrollbar. (5) More breathing room between the search field/results and Seneste anmodninger.

Found the actual cause of the popup that kept looking dim/washed-out across 1.5.0.0 and 1.5.1.0, despite the background genuinely being solid: Jellyfin's own core JS auto-attaches a Web Animations API entrance animation (opacity 0 to 1, scale .96 to 1, 180ms) to any element matching the native dialog class pattern, independent of whether it was opened through Jellyfin's real dialogHelper. Confirmed live via dialog.getAnimations() that this animation gets stuck permanently at its 0%-progress frame - opacity:0 - and never completes, leaving the entire popup invisible/near-invisible instead of just dim. The dialog now proactively cancels this animation right after being inserted (with a couple of staggered retries, since Jellyfin attaches it asynchronously), which reverts the element to its normal CSS-resolved opacity:1 instantly. Also dropped the dialog-fixedSize class the popup didn't actually need (it duplicates constraints already set with !important) - as a side effect this also stopped the ElegantFin theme's own dialog-fixedSize override from forcing square corners on the popup at most window sizes. Two smaller fixes: the Tilføj button now scales up slightly on hover, and items already on the server show a green "Tilføjet check" badge instead of "Tilgaengelig", both in the browse rows and in Seneste anmodninger.

Fix a regression from 1.5.0.0: the new bottom-gradient scrim added a #seerrRequestsTab .cardImageContainer{position:relative} rule to give the ::after scrim a positioning context, but .cardImageContainer is already position:absolute natively (that's what stretches it to fill the aspect-ratio box .cardPadder-overflowPortrait creates via padding-bottom) - our ID-scoped rule had higher specificity than that single-class native rule, silently downgrading it to relative and collapsing every card (Trending/Film/Serier/search/recent-requests) to zero height. Confirmed live: no artwork, no visible or clickable request buttons anywhere in the tab, even though the underlying data and DOM were all correct. Removed the unnecessary position override - position:absolute already gives descendants a valid positioning context, so the scrim didn't need it. Also renamed the request button from "Anmod" to "Tilføj" (and its in-progress/dialog-header/error text to match), for consistency with the "Tilføj Film/Serie" tab button's own wording.

Three fixes based on feedback: (1) Pressing the browser Back button while the Seerr tab was active (e.g. after clicking a Tilgængelig item's link to its Jellyfin details page) left the home page broken, showing only our content and whatever other plugins render outside the tab structure. Root cause: Jellyfin's router only restores the active TAB BUTTON's highlighted state on route re-entry, not the actual is-active class on the content div - that swap normally only happens as a side effect of a real tab-button click, and returning via Back never clicks anything. Now listens for hashchange (not just native tab-button clicks) and explicitly restores whichever native tab's content div matches the currently active button's data-index. (2) The request/season/quality popup looked washed-out - confirmed live its background was only 90% opaque over an already-translucent backdrop, inherited from the theme's own dialog styling meant for bigger dialogs, not a small popup. Forced fully opaque. Also confirmed the popup wasn't reliably centered by the native flex-centering it was relying on, so it's now explicitly centered and capped at min(420px, 92vw) with a tighter phone-width layout, instead of stretching toward the native dialog's full width on smaller windows. (3) Light visual pass to blend Seerr's own look with the Jellyfin theme: consistent indigo accent color across the request button, active genre pills, search focus ring, and popup borders; small accent bars on section titles; a bottom gradient scrim on every poster so badges/buttons stay legible against bright art.

Feedback round: (1) The Anmod button is now a rounded indigo pill centered at the bottom of the poster, matching Seerr's own request-button placement/color, instead of the New-Badges-style top-left corner pill (status badges like Tilgængelig/Anmodet stay top-left). (2) Removed the auto-detected 4K-capability check from 1.3.0.0 - it correctly found that this Seerr instance's single Radarr/Sonarr server reports is4k:false and so was silently always skipping the prompt, but the user confirmed that one server actually handles both 1080p and 4K, so the quality popup is now always shown on every request instead of gating on a server flag that doesn't reflect real capability here. (3) TV requests now open a proper season picker first (fetched via a new /tv-seasons/{tmdbId} endpoint, mirroring Seerr's own request modal) with per-season checkboxes, a Vælg alle toggle, and already-available/requested seasons shown disabled - only the checked seasons are sent instead of always requesting seasons:"all".

Major redesign based on feedback: (1) No longer a separate full-page takeover - integrated as a real home-page tab exactly like Favoritter. Confirmed live that Favoritter never changes the URL, it just toggles an is-active class between sibling .tabContent.pageTabContent divs that Jellyfin keeps permanently mounted inside the home page - the browse panel now works the same way, so you never leave the home page at all. Also found and fixed that clicking a native tab (Hjem/Favoritter) doesn't automatically hide a foreign sibling tab-content div, so a click watcher on the tab row now handles that side explicitly. (2) Split the old single filterable 'Udforsk' grid into three separate always-visible horizontally-scrollable rows - Trending, Film, and Serier - matching the native home page's own row style, each with its own contextual genre filter pills instead of one global type toggle. (3) Requesting now prompts for 1080p vs 4K via a small popup dialog, but only when this Seerr instance actually has a 4K-flagged Radarr/Sonarr server configured (checked via a new /quality-options endpoint) - no point asking a question with only one real answer.

Feature round based on feedback: (1) Subtle slow animated gradient on the page background instead of a flat fill. (2) Fixed 'Seneste anmodninger' not lining up with 'Udforsk' - the native emby-scroller used for the recent-requests row carries its own 23px left padding that the plain discover grid doesn't, now overridden. (3) Items marked Tilgængelig now link straight to their Jellyfin details page - Seerr's own MediaInfo already tracks the linked jellyfinMediaId once something becomes available, no extra lookup needed. (4) Anmod button and status badges restyled as small top-left corner pill badges matching New Badges' own NEW ribbon/rank badge look, instead of a full-width bottom bar. (5) Udforsk now supports Load more pagination using Seerr's page/totalPages fields. (6) Added an Alle/Film/Serier filter row plus contextual genre pills (fetched from Seerr's /genres/movie and /genres/tv) to filter the Udforsk suggestions.

Fix: the 'Tilføj Film/Serie' button could vanish permanently after opening the full page and going back. Button injection cached an 'already injected' flag and skipped re-checking once true, but Jellyfin rebuilds the Hjem/Favoritter tab row's contents on any hashchange - including toggling the ?seerrRequests=1 flag on and back off - even though the underlying route (#/home) never changes, silently wiping the button out from under the stale flag. Removed the flag entirely; the DOM presence check itself is cheap enough to just always run.

Two changes based on feedback: (1) Replaced the floating dialog with a real full-page view. The old modal stacked a translucent dialog body (90% opaque) on top of a translucent backdrop (66% opaque) - both native Jellyfin dialog conventions - which compounded into a noticeably dim, washed-out look. The panel now opens as a solid full-viewport page (matching the app's own #101010 background) toggled by a hash flag on the current route (#/home?seerrRequests=1) rather than a real new route, since navigating to an actually-unrecognized hash renders Jellyfin's own 'Side ikke fundet' error page - this way the browser back button and URL both work like real navigation without fighting Jellyfin's router. (2) The 'Tilføj Film/Serie' button was visibly slower to appear than the native Hjem/Favoritter tabs next to it, because button injection was gated behind the same 400ms debounce used for heavier scan work. It's now a plain cheap idempotency check that runs immediately on every DOM mutation instead.

Fix: the 'Tilføj Film/Serie' button never actually appeared next to Hjem/Favoritter - .tabs-viewmenubar turned out to live in Jellyfin's shared app header (.skinHeader), a sibling of .page.homePage, not a descendant of it, so scoping the search to the active home page found nothing. Now searches the document directly (isHomeRoute() already prevents it from firing while some other section's tab row is showing instead). Live-verified end to end after the fix: button renders in the right spot, click doesn't trigger a tab switch, and search/discover/recent-requests all return correctly enriched data.

Fix: every endpoint returning Ok(new JObject{...}) was silently corrupted - ASP.NET Core's default System.Text.Json output formatter has no built-in support for Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject, and since JObject also implements IEnumerable, it got serialized as an empty array instead of an object (confirmed live: {"ok":true,"version":"3.3.0"} came back as {"ok":[],"version":[]}). All JObject responses now go through a small helper that renders them via Newtonsoft's own ToString() as a raw ContentResult instead, the same way the Seerr proxy passthroughs already worked correctly.

Fix: the config page's embedded <script> never actually executed on this server (Jellyfin's dashboard loads plugin config pages via a mechanism that drops inline scripts), so the Save button's submit handler never attached and clicking it fell through to a native form submission - which leaked the Seerr API key into the browser URL as a plaintext query string. The Save/Test connection form no longer uses a <form>/type="submit" at all, and all of its load/save/test-connection logic now lives in inject.js instead (already proven to load reliably, since it's injected into index.html itself rather than fetched as a page fragment).

Initial release. Adds a 'Tilføj Film/Serie' button to the home page tab row that opens a themed panel with search, a 'Seneste anmodninger' (recent requests) row, and an 'Udforsk' (browse/trending) grid, all backed by a Seerr instance configured in the plugin's settings page (base URL + admin API key). Requests are proxied server-side (avoids CORS entirely) and attributed to the requesting household member's own Seerr account via Seerr's Jellyfin-user lookup, when they've linked one - falls back to the shared admin account otherwise.
